LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT EAST WEST BANK'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES, BREACH OF CONTRACT, BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTIES & CONVERSION & FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: OVERRULED AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION AND SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION. AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, DEFENDANT RAISES FACTUAL ISSUES OF CONTRACT INTERPRETATION THAT ARE NOT AMENABLE TO RESOLUTION BY DEMURRER. AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION, IT IS NOT PERMISSIBLE TO ALLEGE A TORT CLAIM THAT IS MERELY A RESTATED CLAIM FOR BREACH OF CONTRACTUAL OBLIGATIONS. (AAS V. SUPERIOR COURT (2000) 24 CAL.4TH 627, 643). ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: NICHOLE RODICH, CSR #11604

MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-26-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. GARY ALABASTER OF THE SAPIRO LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 771-0100 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JOSEPH N. DEMKO OF THE JEFFER MANGELS, BUTLER & MITCHELL LLP, TEL. NO. 984-9676 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS-DEFENDANT BANK OF GUAM, INCORPORATED'S MOTION AND SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS Denied as to the first and second causes of action and granted as to the third and fourth causes of action. The first cause of action is not based in any part on protected activity. The second cause of action is based in part on non-incidental protected activity, but Cross-Complainant has shown the required minimal to avoid dismissal. The third and fourth causes of action are based on protected activity and Cross-Complainant has not shown the require minimal merit to avoid dismissal. Refusing to consent to the sale of the loans was within Cross-Defendant's rights under the Loan Participation Agreement. Cross-Defendant is awarded attorney's fees pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure 425.16(c)(1). Although Cross-Defendant is only successful in striking two out of four causes of action, it is the prevailing party because the results of the motion were not so insignificant that defendant did not achieve any practical benefit from bringing the motion. However, Cross-Defendant is only entitled to fees in the amount of $7562.50, which is an amount commensurate with the extent that the motion changed the nature and character of the lawsuit in a practical way. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077 = (302/HEK)

MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-05-2012 Continued To Mar-26-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-05-2012 Continued To Mar-19-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
CROSS DEFENDANT BANK OF GUAM, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT is Continued to March 19, 2012 to provide the moving parties the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6C. A courtesy copy of the Cross-Complaint shall be delivered to Department 302 by 4pm March 7, 2012 with a cover letter stating the new hearing date. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR 7077

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-01-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT EAST WEST BANK'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES, BREACH OF CONTRACT, BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTIES & CONVERSION & FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF.
Appearances: Gary Alabaster of the Sapiro Law Firm (771.0100) for plaintiff and responding party; Robert B. Kaplan of Jeffer Mangels Butler & Mitchell LLP (984.9673) for defendant and moving party.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Overruled as to the second cause of action and sustained without leave to amend as to the third cause of action. As to the second cause of action, Defendant raises factual issues of contract interpretation that are not amenable to resolution by demurrer. As to the third cause of action, it is not permissible to allege a tort claim that is merely a restated claim for breach of contractual obligations. (Aas v. Superior Court (2000) 24 Cal.4th 627, 643). Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Nichole Rodich, CSR #11604
